2604.10495.title: Why Don't You Know? Evaluating the Impact of Uncertainty Sources on Uncertainty Quantification in LLMs
2604.10495.paper_summary: As Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in real-world applications, reliable uncertainty quantification (UQ) becomes critical for safe and effective use. Most existing UQ approaches for language models aim to produce a single confidence score -- for example, estimating the probability that a model's answer is correct. However, uncertainty in natural language tasks arises from multiple distinct sources, including model knowledge gaps, output variability, and input ambiguity, which have different implications for system behavior and user interaction. In this work, we study how the source of uncertainty impacts the behavior and effectiveness of existing UQ methods. To enable controlled analysis, we introduce a new dataset that explicitly categorizes uncertainty sources, allowing systematic evaluation of UQ performance under each condition. Our experiments reveal that while many UQ methods perform well when uncertainty stems solely from model knowledge limitations, their performance degrades or becomes misleading when other sources are introduced. These findings highlight the need for uncertainty-aware methods that explicitly account for the source of uncertainty in large language models.
2604.10495.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）在現實世界應用中的日益普及，可靠的不確定性量化（UQ）對於安全和有效的使用變得至關重要。大多數現有的語言模型UQ方法旨在產生單一的信心分數——例如，估計模型答案正確的概率。然而，自然語言任務中的不確定性來自多個不同的來源，包括模型知識差距、輸出變異性和輸入歧義，這些來源對系統行為和用戶互動有不同的影響。在本研究中，我們探討不確定性來源如何影響現有UQ方法的行為和有效性。為了進行控制分析，我們引入了一個新數據集，明確分類不確定性來源，允許在每種條件下系統評估UQ性能。我們的實驗顯示，儘管許多UQ方法在不確定性僅源於模型知識限制時表現良好，但當引入其他來源時，它們的性能會下降或變得誤導性。這些發現突顯了需要不確定性感知方法，這些方法明確考慮大型語言模型中的不確定性來源。
2604.10495.author: Maiya Goloburda et.al.
2604.10495.authors: Maiya Goloburda, Roman Vashurin, Fedor Chernogorsky, Nurkhan Laiyk, Daniil Orel, Preslav Nakov, Maxim Panov
